message.content:
You are diagnosing a real bug for a careful refactor. Tools: Bash, Read, Grep. Work in /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent.

QUESTION: Where is the "checklist" / gap-ledger logic that decides "grounded deficiencies" in the outline agent? Read it in /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/src/polaris_graph/outline/. Why would it return "NONE (no grounded deficiencies)" for a genuine CV-safety coverage gap? Quote the decision logic with file:line and give the most likely reason it under-detected.

Actually READ the code (don't guess). Write your findings to /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/search_diag/gap_detection.md (concrete, with file:line quotes + a clear conclusion). Reply ONLY: "gap_detection: <one-line conclusion>".

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: The core logic is in `_run_checklist` (line 1354). Let me read it in detail, plus the grounding gate around line 1500-1620.

content.content:
1354	    async def _run_checklist(self, trigger: str) -> list[GapTodo]:
1355	        """LLM self-review, PER SECTION: exhaustive coverage + information density +
1356	        compute-need. Mirrors ``fs_researcher_query_gen.plan_fs_researcher_queries``'s checklist
1357	        critic (same status-leak screen), scoped to the current outline draft.
1358	
1359	        Iter-2 P0-1 fix (saturated negative control invented gaps on a single-fact question):
1360	        every deficiency line now REQUIRES a verbatim quote from the research question that
1361	        names or directly implies the claimed missing aspect. A line whose quote does not
1362	        literally appear in the question text is mechanically dropped — never silently kept on
1363	        trust. This is the same grounding philosophy strict_verify already applies to generated
1364	        prose (claim must trace to cited text), applied here to the GAP-DETECTION step itself so
1365	        the loop cannot manufacture retrieval work the question never asked for. Question-
1366	        agnostic: no domain/topic vocabulary is hardcoded, only the require-a-literal-quote
1367	        discipline.
1368	
1369	        Iter-4 P0-1 fix (Fable-confirmed real-corpus finding: on the locked 4-section DRB-72
1370	        AI-labor deliverable this checklist proposed EXACTLY ONE recurring candidate — a
1371	        phantom 'Summary Table' section that can never be homed under an exact-N-in-order
1372	        contract — and NOTHING ELSE, on both the full and a deliberately-thinned corpus. The
1373	        model was reading the question's cross-cutting-table sentence as its OWN section
1374	        instead of decomposing it into per-section facets. The prompt now (a) closes the
1375	        <section title> field to an EXACT copy of a listed current-outline title and explicitly
1376	        instructs decomposition of any table/summary/cross-cutting request into per-section
1377	        facets, with a worked example, and (b) as a mechanical backstop independent of prompt
1378	        compliance, ``_best_remap_title`` routes any section label that still doesn't resolve
1379	        to the existing section whose own topic (title+focus) the facet text overlaps most,
1380	        before ever falling back to an unhomeable veto."""
1381	        if not self.workspace.outline_draft:
1382	            return []
1383	        from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import OpenRouterClient  # noqa: PLC0415
1384	        from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.fs_researcher_query_gen import (  # noqa: PLC0415
1385	            _screen_status_lines,
1386	        )
1387	
1388	        section_block = "\n".join(
1389	            f"- {_plan_field(p, 'title', '')!r} (focus: {str(_plan_field(p, 'focus', ''))[:100]!r}, "
1390	            f"{len(_plan_field(p, 'ev_ids', []) or [])} sources)"
1391	            for p in self.workspace.outline_draft
1392	        )
1393	        title_list = ", ".join(
1394	            repr(str(_plan_field(p, "title", "") or "")) for p in self.workspace.outline_draft
1395	        )
1396	        prompt = (
1397	            "Self-review this outline against the research question. For EACH section, check: "
1398	            "(a) exhaustive coverage — any aspect of that section's focus the assigned sources "
1399	            "cannot answer?, (b) information density — any aspect backed by only 1-2 sources?, "
1400	            "(c) compute need — does the section's focus imply a numeric comparison that has no "
1401	            "supporting data?\n\n"
1402	            "SECTION FIELD RULE (read carefully): the <section title> field of every line you "
1403	            f"write MUST be an EXACT character-for-character copy of one of these existing "
1404	            f"outline titles: {title_list}. Never write a section title that is not in that "
1405	            "list — do not invent a new section, and do not name a separate deliverable-level "
1406	            "artifact (a standalone summary table, an appendix, a references list, a "
1407	            "conclusion) as if it were its own section. If the QUESTION asks for something "
1408	            "that would naturally live in such a cross-cutting artifact (e.g. 'create a "
1409	            "summary table covering application cases, impacts, and risks'), DECOMPOSE it: "
1410	            "write one deficiency line PER underlying facet and assign each line to whichever "
1411	            "EXISTING section above is topically closest to that facet (a facet about risks or "
1412	            "limitations goes under whichever section already discusses risks/challenges; a "
1413	            "facet about applications, industries, or opportunities goes under whichever "
1414	            "section already discusses applications/opportunities; a facet about outcomes or "
1415	            "effects goes under whichever section already discusses impacts/findings). Every "
1416	            "existing section is a valid home for SOME facet — a table/summary request is "
1417	            "never, by itself, a reason to list zero deficiencies.\n\n"
1418	            "STRICT GROUNDING RULE (read carefully — this is a precision gate, not a recall "
1419	            "gate): only flag a deficiency for an aspect that is EXPLICITLY named or directly "
1420	            "implied by the wording of the QUESTION below. Do NOT invent generic sub-topics, "
1421	            "background, history, mechanisms, or comparisons just because they would be "
1422	            "'interesting to know' about the subject — if the question does not ask for it, it "
1423	            "is not a deficiency. When you list a deficiency you MUST include a QUOTE: a short "
1424	            "verbatim excerpt copied EXACTLY (same words, same characters, no added quotation "
1425	            "marks of your own around it) from the QUESTION text that names the specific "
1426	            "missing facet. A quote that is just the subject's name (e.g. only the topic noun) "
1427	            "does NOT count — it must name the FACET (e.g. 'long-term cardiovascular safety', "
1428	            "'compared with other agents', 'in specific populations', 'particular industries "
1429	            "or occupations', 'key risk points'). If you cannot produce such a quote, do not "
1430	            "list the line.\n\n"
1431	            "If the question is a single, narrow, already-answered factual question, or every "
1432	            "section is fully adequate, reply exactly NONE and list nothing else.\n\n"
1433	            "Format each deficiency as ONE line, four fields separated by ' :: ':\n"
1434	            "<section title, copied EXACTLY from the list above> :: <specific missing aspect> "
1435	            ":: <coverage|density|numeric_rows> :: <verbatim quote from QUESTION>\n\n"
1436	            "Example (question asks two things, sources only cover one) — CORRECT:\n"
1437	            "Safety :: long-term cardiovascular safety :: coverage :: long-term cardiovascular "
1438	            "safety\n"
1439	            "Example (question asks for a cross-cutting summary table of application cases and "
1440	            "risks across industries; outline has sections 'Opportunities' and 'Challenges') — "
1441	            "CORRECT (decomposed into 2 lines, each under an EXISTING section):\n"
1442	            "Opportunities :: specific application cases by industry or occupation :: coverage "
1443	            ":: particular industries or occupations\n"
1444	            "Challenges :: key risk points emphasized by researchers :: coverage :: key risk "
1445	            "points emphasized by researchers\n"
1446	            "WRONG for the same question: 'Summary Table :: application cases, impacts, and "
1447	            "risks by industry :: coverage :: summary table' — this invents a section that "
1448	            "isn't in the list above instead of decomposing into the existing sections.\n"
1449	            "Example (single-fact question, already answered) — CORRECT reply: NONE\n"
1450	            "Example of what NOT to do: inventing 'engineering mechanisms' or 'other tall "
1451	            "structures' for a question that only asks a completion year — WRONG, do not do "
1452	            "this.\n\n"
1453	            f"QUESTION:\n{self.workspace.research_question}\n\nSECTIONS:\n{section_block}"
1454	        )
1455	        checklist_max_tokens = _env_int(
1456	            "PG_OUTLINE_CHECKLIST_MAX_TOKENS", PG_OUTLINE_CHECKLIST_MAX_TOKENS_DEFAULT,
1457	        )
1458	        # Iter-6 P0 fix (Fable-directed, both halves REQUIRED):
1459	        #   (1) root cause — pass an explicit ``reasoning_max_tokens`` so glm-5.2's
1460	        #       unbounded-effort reasoning pool is BOUNDED within the (now much larger)
1461	        #       total, instead of running unbounded until it eats the whole budget and
1462	        #       leaves content empty (the exact ReasoningFirstTruncationError trace from
1463	        #       matched_comparison_run5.log — same starved defaults iters 1-5 never fixed).
1464	        #   (2) defense in depth — this is an INTERNAL control-plane call (a self-review, not
1465	        #       verified prose); a transient truncation here must never be able to abort the
1466	        #       whole outline stage. One retry at 2x budget (capped at the real glm-5.2
1467	        #       provider ceiling), then fail-OPEN to "no new deficiencies this round" with a
1468	        #       disclosed reason — mirrors the fail-open pattern already used by
1469	        #       ``_tool_search_more_evidence``'s query-derive call just below.
1470	        resp = None
1471	        last_exc: Optional[Exception] = None
1472	        for attempt, attempt_max_tokens in enumerate((
1473	            checklist_max_tokens,
1474	            min(checklist_max_tokens * 2, PG_GLM52_REAL_MAX_COMPLETION_TOKENS),
1475	        )):
1476	            client = OpenRouterClient(model=self.agent_model)
1477	            try:
1478	                resp = await client.generate(
1479	                    prompt=prompt,
1480	                    max_tokens=attempt_max_tokens,
1481	                    temperature=0.2,
1482	                    reasoning_max_tokens=_reasoning_max_tokens(),
1483	                )
1484	                last_exc = None
1485	                break
1486	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — a checklist truncation must degrade, never crash
1487	                last_exc = exc
1488	                logger.warning(
1489	                    "[outline_agent] checklist call failed (trigger=%s, attempt=%d, "
1490	                    "max_tokens=%d): %s", trigger, attempt + 1, attempt_max_tokens, exc,
1491	                )
1492	            finally:
1493	                if hasattr(client, "close"):
1494	                    try:
1495	                        await client.close()
1496	                    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
1497	                        pass
1498	        if resp is None:
1499	            self.workspace.disclose(
1500	                f"checklist (trigger={trigger}) degraded fail-open after "
1501	                f"{attempt + 1} attempt(s), treating as 'no new deficiencies this round': "
1502	                f"{last_exc}"
1503	            )
1504	            return []
1505	
1506	        raw_lines = [
1507	            ln.strip("- ").strip() for ln in (resp.content or "").splitlines() if ln.strip()
1508	        ]
1509	        screened = _screen_status_lines(raw_lines, self.workspace.research_question)
1510	        question_norm = _normalize_for_quote_check(self.workspace.research_question)
1511	        new_todos: list[GapTodo] = []
1512	        n_ungrounded = 0
1513	        ungrounded_lines: list[str] = []
1514	        n_unhomeable = 0
1515	        unhomeable_lines: list[str] = []
1516	        n_remapped = 0
1517	        remapped_lines: list[str] = []
1518	        for line in screened[:12]:
1519	            if line.upper().strip() == "NONE":
1520	                continue
1521	            parts = [p.strip() for p in line.split("::")]
1522	            if len(parts) < 4:
1523	                # iter-2: a line without the mandatory quote field is UNGROUNDED by
1524	                # construction — never kept on trust (fail-closed, not fail-open — this is
1525	                # the precision gate the P0-1 fix requires, the mirror image of the
1526	                # fail-open junk-deletion carve-out which governs a different job).
1527	                n_ungrounded += 1
1528	                ungrounded_lines.append(line[:160])
1529	                continue
1530	            section, aspect, kind_raw, quote = parts[0], parts[1], parts[2], parts[3]
1531	            if not _quote_is_grounded(quote, question_norm):
1532	                n_ungrounded += 1
1533	                ungrounded_lines.append(line[:160])
1534	                continue
1535	            kind = kind_raw if kind_raw in ("coverage", "density", "numeric_rows") else "coverage"
1536	            # Iter-3 P0 fix (real-corpus degeneration): a gap whose named section resolves to
1537	            # NO current outline title, under a LOCKED required-structure deliverable (no room
1538	            # to add a new section), can never be routed to retrieval — it is a deliverable-
1539	            # level/formatting request (e.g. "Summary Table"), not a content gap. This is a
1540	            # STRUCTURAL check (does the section resolve?), not a keyword/domain-vocabulary
1541	            # guess, so it stays question-agnostic.
1542	            #
1543	            # Iter-4 P0-1 fix: before recording it UNFILLED, try `_best_remap_title` — a
1544	            # mechanical (never-LLM) backstop that routes the facet to whichever EXISTING
1545	            # section its own vocabulary overlaps most, independent of whether the prompt-level
1546	            # decomposition instruction above was actually followed. This is what turns a
1547	            # phantom-section "Summary Table" line into a genuine, retrievable content gap
1548	            # against a real locked section instead of a permanent veto.
1549	            if self.workspace.required_titles and self._resolve_section_title(section) is None:
1550	                remap_title = self._best_remap_title(aspect)
1551	                if remap_title is not None:
1552	                    n_remapped += 1
1553	                    remapped_lines.append(f"{section!r}->{remap_title!r}::{aspect[:60]}")
1554	                    section = remap_title
1555	                else:
1556	                    self.workspace.gap_ledger.add_unfillable(
1557	                        section=section, aspect=aspect,
1558	                        reason=(
1559	                            f"named section {section!r} does not match any of the locked "
1560	                            f"required section titles {self.workspace.required_titles!r}, a new "
1561	                            "section cannot be added under this deliverable's exact-structure "
1562	                            "contract, and no existing section's own topic overlaps this facet's "
1563	                            "vocabulary enough to remap it — this reads as a deliverable-level/"
1564	                            "formatting request, not a retrievable content gap; recorded as a "
1565	                            "structural limitation, not retried"
1566	                        ),
1567	                        needed_kind=kind, source="checklist",
1568	                    )
1569	                    # Iter-3 P1 fix: bank the SECTION LABEL itself (not the (section,aspect)
1570	                    # pair) as proven-unhomeable — see `OutlineWorkspace.unhomeable_sections`
1571	                    # docstring. A real THINNED-corpus run showed the checklist re-wording the
1572	                    # ASPECT text each round (dodging the (section,aspect) paraphrase dedup)
1573	                    # while the SECTION label stayed constant; vetoing by section label closes
1574	                    # that gap.
1575	                    self.workspace.unhomeable_sections.add(_normalize_section_label(section))
1576	                    n_unhomeable += 1
1577	                    unhomeable_lines.append(f"{section}::{aspect}")
1578	                    continue
1579	            todo = self.workspace.gap_ledger.add(
1580	                section=section, aspect=aspect, needed_kind=kind, source="checklist",
1581	            )
1582	            new_todos.append(todo)
1583	        if new_todos:
1584	            self.workspace.disclose(
1585	                f"checklist[{trigger}] named {len(new_todos)} gap(s): "
1586	                + "; ".join(f"{t.section}::{t.aspect}" for t in new_todos[:6])
1587	            )
1588	        if n_remapped:
1589	            self.workspace.disclose(
1590	                f"checklist[{trigger}] remapped {n_remapped} facet(s) from a section label "
1591	                "that did not match any locked required title onto the existing section its "
1592	                "vocabulary overlaps most (iter-4 P0-1 fix — routes content instead of vetoing "
1593	                "a format-only request): " + "; ".join(remapped_lines[:6])
1594	            )
1595	        if n_unhomeable:
1596	            self.workspace.disclose(
1597	                f"checklist[{trigger}] recorded {n_unhomeable} unhomeable gap(s) as UNFILLED "
1598	                "(no matching section under the locked required-structure deliverable — "
1599	                "structural limitation, never retried): " + "; ".join(unhomeable_lines[:6])
1600	            )
1601	        if n_ungrounded:
1602	            # §-1.1 auditability fix: the OLD disclosure was a bare count — a reader could never
1603	            # verify whether the anti-invention gate correctly rejected an invented sub-topic or
1604	            # wrongly swallowed a REAL gap that just phrased its quote loosely. Surface the actual
1605	            # dropped line text (truncated) so a line-by-line read can judge each one.
1606	            self.workspace.disclose(
1607	                f"checklist[{trigger}] dropped {n_ungrounded} ungrounded line(s) "
1608	                "(no verbatim question quote — anti-invention gate, P0-1 fix): "
1609	                + " | ".join(ungrounded_lines[:6])
1610	            )
1611	        if not new_todos and not n_ungrounded and not n_unhomeable:
1612	            # Iter-2 telemetry fix: previously a genuine "the checklist ran and found NOTHING"
1613	            # outcome (the correct behavior on a saturated/fully-covered outline) left ZERO trace
1614	            # in `disclosures` — indistinguishable from the checklist never having been invoked at
1615	            # all. That ambiguity made the W1 saturated acceptance test unable to tell "loop
1616	            # legitimately declined to search" apart from "loop never got far enough to check".
1617	            # Always disclose the checklist having run, even on a clean NONE verdict.
1618	            self.workspace.disclose(f"checklist[{trigger}] ran: NONE (no grounded deficiencies)")
1619	        return new_todos
1620	
1621	    # -- detector 2: tool-failure deterministic ----------------------------
1622	
1623	    def _tool_failure_gap_check(self, step: AnalysisStep) -> None:

content.text: Now let me look at the grounding check functions `_quote_is_grounded` and `_normalize_for_quote_check`, which are the mechanical filter that decides whether a line survives.

content.content:
292:def _normalize_for_quote_check(text: str) -> str:
298:def _quote_is_grounded(quote: str, question_norm: str) -> bool:
307:    q = _normalize_for_quote_check(quote)
1385:            _screen_status_lines,
1509:        screened = _screen_status_lines(raw_lines, self.workspace.research_question)
1510:        question_norm = _normalize_for_quote_check(self.workspace.research_question)
1531:            if not _quote_is_grounded(quote, question_norm):

283	# iter-2 P0-1 fix: the checklist's anti-invention grounding gate. A candidate deficiency line
284	# must carry a verbatim quote from the research question; these two helpers are the mechanical
285	# (never-LLM) check that the quote is real, not a paraphrase or a hallucinated snippet.
286	_MIN_GROUNDING_QUOTE_WORDS = 2
287	
288	
289	_QUOTE_WRAP_CHARS = "\"'‘’“”‹›«»`"
290	
291	
292	def _normalize_for_quote_check(text: str) -> str:
293	    import re as _re
294	    stripped = str(text or "").strip().strip(_QUOTE_WRAP_CHARS).strip()
295	    return _re.sub(r"\s+", " ", stripped.lower())
296	
297	
298	def _quote_is_grounded(quote: str, question_norm: str) -> bool:
299	    """True iff ``quote`` (whitespace/case-normalized, and with any wrapping quote-mark
300	    PUNCTUATION the model added around its own answer stripped — iter-4 robustness fix: a
301	    model reply like the literal three characters ``"foo bar"`` around an otherwise-correct
302	    verbatim excerpt must not be mechanically rejected just because those wrapping marks are
303	    not themselves part of the source text) is a literal substring of the (already-normalized)
304	    research question AND carries at least ``_MIN_GROUNDING_QUOTE_WORDS`` words (a bare
305	    one-word quote like the subject's name is not a specific-enough justification for a
306	    claimed missing FACET)."""
307	    q = _normalize_for_quote_check(quote)
308	    if not q or len(q.split()) < _MIN_GROUNDING_QUOTE_WORDS:
309	        return False
310	    return q in question_norm
311	
312	
313	@dataclass
314	class GapTodo:
315	    section: str
316	    aspect: str
317	    needed_kind: str = "coverage"   # "coverage" | "numeric_rows" | "density"
318	    status: str = "PENDING"          # PENDING | IN_PROGRESS | COMPLETE | UNFILLED
319	    attempts: int = 0
320	    source: str = "checklist"        # checklist | tool_failure | agent
321	    disclosure: str = ""
322
